questions.question: The standard meridian of India is:
questions.explanation: The standard meridian of India is 82°30'E (passing through Mirzapur, UP), which is used to determine Indian Standard Time (IST = UTC+5:30).

questions.question: The Neyveli Lignite Corporation (NLC) mines lignite for power generation in:
questions.explanation: Neyveli Lignite Corporation (NLC India Ltd) is located in Cuddalore district (formerly South Arcot), Tamil Nadu. It mines lignite (brown coal) and operates thermal power plants. Neyveli has Asia's largest opencast lignite mines.

questions.question: India's largest iron ore reserves are in:
options: Jharkhand and Odisha (Jharkhand-Odisha iron ore belt)
questions.explanation: The Jharkhand-Odisha iron ore belt (Singhbhum, Keonjhar, Mayurbhanj) has India's largest iron ore reserves. Other important areas: Karnataka (Bellary, Sandur), Chhattisgarh (Bastar), Goa. India has ~5.5 billion tonnes of iron ore reserves.

questions.question: The 'Oil and Natural Gas Corporation' (ONGC) produces most of India's petroleum from:
options: Mumbai High (offshore, Arabian Sea) — about 60% of production
questions.explanation: Mumbai High (Bombay High) offshore oilfields in the Arabian Sea produce about 60% of India's total crude oil. Other producing areas: Gujarat (Ankleshwar, Kalol), Assam (Digboi — oldest oilfield in Asia, 1889), Rajasthan (Barmer/Mangala).

questions.question: Neyveli Lignite Corporation is in:
questions.explanation: Neyveli Lignite Corporation (now NLC India Limited) is in Neyveli, Cuddalore district, Tamil Nadu. It mines lignite (brown coal) and operates thermal power stations generating electricity primarily for Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: The Kolar Gold Fields (KGF) are in:
questions.explanation: Kolar Gold Fields (KGF) are in Kolar district, Karnataka. Once the world's deepest gold mines, they produced gold for over 120 years before being closed in 2001 by the government due to non-viability.

questions.question: Singareni Coalfields are in:
questions.explanation: Singareni Collieries Company Limited (SCCL) operates coal mines in Telangana (formerly Andhra Pradesh). Singareni is the only government-owned coal company owned jointly by the State (51%) and Centre (49%).

questions.question: Neyveli Lignite (brown coal) is found in:
questions.explanation: Neyveli in Cuddalore district, Tamil Nadu has the largest deposits of lignite (brown coal) in India. NLC India Limited (formerly Neyveli Lignite Corporation) mines it and generates electricity.

questions.question: Which state is the leading producer of petroleum (crude oil) in India?
questions.explanation: Gujarat is the leading producer of crude oil in India, with major offshore fields in the Arabian Sea (Bombay High, operated by ONGC) and onshore fields in Ankleshwar. Rajasthan (Barmer) has emerged as a major onshore producer.

questions.question: The Montreal Protocol (1987) aimed to phase out:
options: Chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs) that deplete the ozone layer
questions.explanation: The Montreal Protocol on Substances that Deplete the Ozone Layer (1987) is a landmark treaty phasing out CFCs (chlorofluorocarbons) and other ozone-depleting substances. It is considered the most successful environmental treaty.

questions.question: Evergreen Revolution aims at:
options: Sustainable increase in agricultural productivity without environmental damage
questions.explanation: Evergreen Revolution (concept by M.S. Swaminathan) aims at sustainable increase in agricultural productivity without ecological harm — combining the productivity of the Green Revolution with environmental sustainability.

questions.question: OPEC (Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries) headquarters is in:
questions.explanation: OPEC is headquartered in Vienna, Austria. Founded in Baghdad in 1960, it currently has 13 member countries. Saudi Arabia is the de facto leader. OPEC controls about 40% of world oil production.

questions.question: OPEC (Organisation of Petroleum Exporting Countries) was founded in:
questions.explanation: OPEC was founded in 1960 in Baghdad by Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Venezuela. Today it has 13 members. OPEC controls ~40% of world oil production. Its secretariat is in Vienna, Austria.

questions.question: The Gulf of Kutch is famous for:
options: Tidal energy potential, Kandla Port, and flamingos
questions.explanation: Gulf of Kutch (Gujarat) has one of the highest tidal ranges in India (7-8 m), making it ideal for tidal energy. Kandla Port (Deendayal Port) is India's second-largest port by cargo. The gulf also has flamingos and corals.
